angle: Lane C/D adapter boundary and external pilot

finding.angle: Lane C/D adapter boundary and external pilot
finding.recommendation: Make Lane C (`skill-benchmark`) a first-class mode of `deep-loop-workflows`; make Lane D (`non-dev-ai-system-refine`) a first-class public mode/command entry but keep it explicitly adapter-backed, with the Python loop and kill-switch logic owned by each external packaging. Do not nest both under an opaque improvement mode, do not exclude self-benchmarking, and do not move Lane D logic into `deep-loop-runtime` or the merged workflow skill. Handle the merged-skill self-reference by benchmark-by-mode: D1-inter may validate advisor selection of `deep-loop-workflows`, but D1-intra/D2 must validate mode-specific intent/resource routing. No external Barter contract-version bump is required for a path/docs-only skill rename; coordinate only doc/comment updates unless argv/env/journal/kill-switch semantics change.
key_tradeoffs: First-class Lane C/D routing preserves per-mode behavior and advisor precision, but increases alias/mode metadata work in the merged skill.
Keeping Lane D adapter-only preserves the external pilot contract and frozen runtime boundary, but the merged skill remains dependent on packaging-owned Python loops for real behavior.
Benchmark-by-mode keeps the merged skill testable without false whole-skill passes, but requires mode-scoped playbooks or scenario filters.
No contract bump minimizes Barter churn, but stale external comments/docs must still be coordinated to avoid operator confusion.
risks: Lane C can falsely pass self-benchmarking if mode-specific resource/intent checks are not required for `deep-loop-workflows`.
Lane C contamination lint can reject self-benchmark prompts that leak merged skill or old mode vocabulary.
Lane D can be broken by accidentally changing env/argv/journal/kill-switch semantics during a path-only move.
Advisor alias collapse can route to the merged skill while losing mode intent.
The documented Barter pilot path is stale; the mounted path is `Barter - Copywriter`.
depends_on: Q-ARCH final internal layout for deep-loop-workflows
Q-BACKEND stop/journal taxonomy ownership if hoisted
open_questions: Will `deep-loop-workflows` store old subtrees flat under the new root or under `modes/<mode>` with compatibility wrappers?
Can the skill advisor expose mode attribution, or must Lane C reports carry mode precision entirely through D1-intra/resource scoring?
Should Barter receive a docs-only kit_version bump, or just non-contract comment/doc updates?
